titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale School of Drama Master of Fine Arts Theses
Yale School of Drama Master of Fine Arts Theses
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Hannah Rae Montgomery

publicationstmt.publisher: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9653148.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 13, 2025 at 19:00

did.unittitle: Yale School of Drama Master of Fine Arts Theses

corpname.part: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

did.abstract: This collection contains thesis projects submitted in partial fulfillment for the degree of Master of Fine Arts at the Yale School of Drama.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.
Written permission of the author is required for copying.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale School of Drama Master of Fine Arts Theses, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains thesis projects submitted in partial fulfillment for the degree of Master of Fine Arts at the Yale School of Drama.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Directing. II. Stage Management. III. Technical Design and Production. IV. Miscellaneous.

did.unittitle: Directing Theses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes production books from third-year director's thesis projects, in partial fulfillment of requirements for the Master of Fine Arts degree in Directing at the Yale School of Drama.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series is arranged chronologically by year of completion, then alphabetically by author surname within each year.

did.unittitle: Saint Mac by John Kneubuhl. Production book, with essay, by Virginia H. Mealy
The Inquilines; a play / written and directed by Vincent Park.
Come to the Party
The Government Inspector by Nikolai Gogol. Production book of YSD production Jan/Feb, 1977
Director's thesis on Euripides' Alcestis .
Models of life : a director's thesis.
Happy end : a production casebook / Eleanor Holdridge.
The Crazy Locomotive by Stanislaw Ignacy Witkiewicz / directed by Annie Dorsen
The People vs. The God of Vengeance by Scholem Asch and Rebecca Bayla Taichman / directed by Rebecca Bayla Taichman
The Life and Death of King John by William Shakespeare / directed by Stephen Fried
Hamlet written by William Shakespeare / adapted and directed by May Adrales
The Duchess of Malfi by John Webster / directed by Susanna Gellert
Attempts on Her Life by Martin Crimp / directed by Anna Jones
Zero Hour / written and directed by Tea Alagic
Baal by Bertolt Brecht / directed by Snehal Desai

Persecution and assassination of Jean-Paul Marat as performed by the inmates of the Asylum of Charenton under the direction of the Marquis of Sade / English version by Geoffrey Skelton Verse adaptation by Adrian Mitchell / directed by Nelson T. Eusebio, III
Venus by Suzan-Lori Parks / directed by Jessi D. Hill
Peer Gynt by Henrik Ibsen / directed by Mike Donahue
The Ghost Sonata by August Strindberg / directed by Shana Cooper
The Robbers by Friedrich Schiller / directed by Rebecca Wolff
La Ronde by Arthur Schnitzler, translated by Carl R. Mueller
Phèdre by Jean Racine, translated by Ted Hughes / directed by Christopher Mirto
Man=Man by Erik Pearson
Orlando by Sarah Ruhl / directed by Jen Wineman
Eurydice by Jean Anouilh / directed by Devin Brain
A Streetcar Named Desire by Tennessee Williams / directed by Charlotte Brathwaite
Jib by Michael McQuilken / directed by Michael McQuilken
Doctor Faustus Lights the Lights by Gertrude Stein / directed by Lileana Blain-Cruz
The Seagull by Anton Chekov / directed by Alexandru Mihail
Cymbeline by William Shakespeare / directed by Louisa Proske
Cloud Nine by Caryl Churchill / directed by Margot Bordelon
The master and margarita / by Mikhail Bulgakov / adapted by Edward Kemp, directed by Sara Holdren at Yale School of Drama, October 21-25, 2014.
Friedrich Dürrenmatt's "The Visit" / presented at the Iseman Theater on October 29th through November 2nd 2014.
Hedda Gabler / director's casebook for Henrik Ibsen's Hedda Gabler presented at the University Theater at Yale School of Drama on February 1-7, 2014.
Don Juan / by Molière, translated by Brendan Pelsue, adapted by Andrej Visky, Brendan Pelsue, and Samantha Lazar. Yale School of Drama January 27-31, 2015.
Tartuffe. Translated by Richard Wilbur. Production book, with essay (4 pp.) and bibliography (1 p.) by Thomas Turgeon.
Uncle Vanya by Anton Chekhov, Directed by Gia Forakis
Stage Management Theses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes theses by third-year stage managers, in partial fulfillment of requirements for the Master of Fine Arts degree in Stage Management at the Yale School of Drama.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is divided into two subseries: I. Production Theses (acting editions/production books for productions staged); and II. Written Theses (research and critical analysis on a faculty-approved topic in theatre history or practice). Both subseries are arranged chronologically by year of completion, then alphabetically by author surname within each year.

did.unittitle: Production Theses
Sunset crowd / by John Kneubuhl ; a production book
The Tempest by William Shakespeare : acting edition: Charlene Harrington, vol. I & vol. II
Shlemiel the first / by Isaac Bashevis Singer ; acting edition by Peter Roberts.
Suicide in B-Flat ( A Mysterious Overture) by Sam Shepard: acting edition
Wings by Arthur Kopit: acting edition
The Psychic Life of Savages by Amy Freed: acting edition by Laura J. Mac Neil
The Mystery Plays by Roberto Aguirre-Sacasa : acting edition
A Path to Leverage Stage Management Expertise into a Production Management Career by Sterling Michols
The People Next Door by Henry Adams
The Dance of the holy ghosts: a play on memory by Marcus Gardley / directed by Liz Diamond: acting edition: Adam Ganderson
Black Snow by Mikhail Bulgakov / adapted by Keith Reddin : acting edition
Lulu by Frank Wedekind / translated by Carl Mueller / adapted by Mark Lamos and Drew Lichtenberg : acting edition
The Mistakes Madeline Made by Elizabeth Meriwether : acting edition
The Evildoers by David Adjmi / directed by Rebecca Taichman
A Woman of No Importance by Oscar Wilde : acting edition
Trouble in Mind by Alice Childress / directed by Irene Lewis
Boleros for the Disenchanted by Jose Rivera / directed by Henry Godinez: acting edition by Danielle Federico
Notes from Underground by Fyodor Dostoevsky / adapted by Robert Woodruff and Bill Camp : acting edition
Rough Crossing by Tom Stoppard / directed by Mark Rucker : acting edition
Happy Now? by Lucinda Coxon / directed by Liz Diamond : acting edition
Compulsion by Rinne Groff / directed by Oskar Eustis : acting edition
Eclipsed by Danai Gurira / directed by Liesl Tommy : acting edition
Bossa Nova by Kirsten Greenidge / directed by Evan Yionoulis: acting edition by Lee Micklin
The Piano Lesson by August Wilson / directed by Liesl Tommy: acting edition by Allison Hall Johnson
The Caucasian Chalk Circle by Bertolt Brecht / directed by Liz Diamond: acting edition by Shannon L. Gaughf
Written Theses
Ruth Gordon, Her Stage Career by Carol Bretz
Creative Arts Centre for Trinidad and Tobago; A Preliminary Study in Functional Design

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box 24 also contains "A Project in Architectural Lighting," which is another work by Lyndersay.

did.unittitle: The performing arts : a proposed union, by Don S. Anderson.
The case for resident repertory theatre in Denver, Colorado.
Additional spaces for existing theatres / by Daniel L. Koetting.
Proposed LORT-Equity agreement governing employment in conservatory theatres / Francis A. Lackner.
The Theatrical Organization: a systems view / by Charles E. Letts, III.
Ballet Stage Management by Kathleen Ellen Cogbill
The Story Keeper: The Evolution of Stage Management in Ancient Greece and Rome
The Relationship Between Stage Manager and Director: Interviews with Professionals in the Field
Stage Management: A Foundation, Career and Lifestyle (Current and Former Stage Managers Share Their Perspectives) by Kirsten Parker
Technical Design and Production Theses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes theses by third-year Technical Design and Production students, in partial fulfillment of requirements for the Master of Fine Arts degree in Technical Design and Production at the Yale School of Drama.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series is arranged chronologically by year of completion, then alphabetically by author surname within each year.

did.unittitle: A Lighting Layout for High School Auditoriums and Stages
Stage Lighting in the Opera House
Dramatic Production in the Private, Secondary Schools of Connecticut
A Graphic Survey of Stage Lighting, vol. I-II
Unistrut Theatre Architecture, A Use Study
Optics for the Theatre Technician
Light and the Music Drama: The Aesthetic Basis and Technical Practice of Stage Lighting at the Bayreuth Festspielhaus
Stage Lighting Specifications for The University of Northern Iowa Theatres
The portable stage light simulator, by Daniel H. Proctor.
Lighting and Sound Systems for the Swimming Pool of the Northfield School
Characteristics of the Yale Spotline Winch, by Craig T. Martin.
The history and technology of pre-electric theatre lighting.
Production problems in regional theaters, Part I, by Kathryn M. Pinner.
The Yale Drama Inventory Management System: A Computer Based Inventory System For Non-Profit Institutions
Acoustical design : Yale Repertory Theatre renovation 1975 / by Mitchell Kurtz.
Modelling basic operating overhead in theatre buildings : a pilot study at three universities / submitted by Jonathan Seth Miller.
Theater rigging : a guide to maintenance inspection / by Thomas J. Kupp.
A hydraulic primer for theatre production personnel / by Andrew J. Kufta.
Management for the technical manager / by William J. Reynolds.
Stress-skin panel design / by Robert C. Heller.

Design of Structural Plates for Theatre
